explanation: All Section 2 criteria are satisfied. 2.1: Confirmed cervical radiculopathy at C5-C6 with nerve root compression and dermatomal symptoms, supported by MRI within 6 months. 2.2(a): 8 weeks of supervised PT directed at the cervical condition with documented functional outcomes (exceeds 6-week minimum). 2.2(b): Two qualifying medications trialed — naproxen (NSAID) for 6 weeks and gabapentin (neuropathic agent) for 5 weeks, both exceeding 4-week minimum. 2.2(c): One cervical epidural steroid injection at C5-C6 satisfies the interventional requirement. 2.3: MRI within 6 months demonstrates structural pathology consistent with symptoms and proposed surgical level, interpreted by a radiologist (policy permits radiologist OR treating specialist). 2.4: Physician provided all required attestations, rationale, functional limitations, and statement of risks/alternatives. Therefore authorization is approved.
